name: Realtek ALC3254 HDA Audio Codec
quirks: Laptop implementations vary by UCM profile, jack wiring, amplifier, DMIC count, and controller driver.
Prefer 48 kHz as the safe automatic graph rate for SOF/HDA laptop paths unless PipeWire exposes a deliberate higher-rate profile.
Match only analog speaker, headphone, headset, and microphone endpoints. HDMI/DisplayPort endpoints on the same SOF/HDA card must use HDMI-specific or generic sink profiles.
Headset, headphones, and digital microphone ports should only be selected when PipeWire marks the port available.
Some hosts expose a deep-buffer playback device; keep low-latency monitoring on the normal HiFi path.

SOF/HDA laptop speaker paths can resync or crackle under small loopback buffers, especially while OBS, webcams, browsers, and effects are active; prefer conservative profile latency over forced ultra-low-latency routing.
Do not write modprobe, SOF, UCM, or kernel-parameter overrides from this profile.
source_notes: Profile identity is the Realtek ALC3254 codec reported by PipeWire/ALSA as alsa.mixer_name, not a specific laptop model.
Observed tuning source: local Alienware m16 R1 exposes Realtek ALC3254 behind Intel 8086:7a50 Raptor Lake HDA with snd_soc_skl_hda_dsp, cfg-dmics:2, and 48 kHz s32le/s24-32le paths.

Local PipeWire speaker path reports api.alsa.period-size=1024, api.alsa.period-num=8, api.alsa.headroom=24, node.max-latency=4096/48000, and spa.alsa resync events when stressed. Revision 4 raises automatic WaveLinux route latency to reduce speaker crackle during ordinary desktop load.
Dell Alienware m16 R1 specifications confirm this implementation has stereo speakers and digital-array microphones, but those platform details are not required for matching.
